_meta.title: PCI DSS v4.0 — Data Protection Requirements
_meta.source: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard v4.0
_meta.description: PCI DSS requirements most relevant to data protection (Req 3, 4, 9, 12)

controls.title: Data retention policy
controls.description: A data retention and disposal policy, procedures, and processes are defined and address all locations of stored account data, including data stored by issuer and companies that support issuing services.

controls.title: Account for stored account data
controls.description: Account data storage is kept to a minimum through implementation of data retention and disposal policies, procedures, and processes.

controls.title: Mask PAN when displayed
controls.description: PAN is masked when displayed (the BIN and last four digits are the maximum number of digits to be displayed).

controls.title: Restrict full PAN access
controls.description: PAN is secured with strong cryptography whenever it is stored electronically.

controls.title: Render PAN unreadable anywhere it is stored
controls.description: PAN is rendered unreadable anywhere it is stored by using strong cryptography such as one-way hashes, truncation, index tokens, or strong encryption.

controls.title: Keyed hashes for rendering PAN unreadable
controls.description: Hashes used to render PAN unreadable are keyed cryptographic hashes of the entire PAN, with effective key-management processes.

controls.title: Disk-level encryption only acceptable in certain scenarios
controls.description: If disk-level or partition-level encryption is used to render PAN unreadable, it is implemented with additional logical access controls to stored data.

controls.title: Key management procedures
controls.description: Procedures are defined and implemented to protect cryptographic keys used to protect stored account data against disclosure and misuse.

controls.title: Strong key generation
controls.description: Key-generating procedures include the use of strong key-generation processes.

controls.title: Secure key distribution
controls.description: Key-distribution procedures include distribution only to authorized custodians.

controls.title: Secure key storage
controls.description: Key-storage procedures are defined, including storage of keys in the fewest possible locations and forms.

controls.title: Cryptographic key changes for keys past crypto-period
controls.description: Cryptographic key changes for keys that have reached the end of their crypto-period, as defined by the associated application vendor or key owner.

requirements.title: Protect Cardholder Data with Strong Cryptography During Transmission

controls.title: Strong cryptography for transmission
controls.description: Strong cryptography and security protocols are implemented to safeguard PAN during transmission over open, public networks.

controls.title: Certificate inventory and trusted keys/certificates
controls.description: An inventory of trusted keys and certificates is maintained.

controls.title: PAN secured with strong cryptography for internal transmission
controls.description: PAN is secured with strong cryptography whenever it is sent via end-user messaging technologies.

requirements.title: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data

controls.title: Media physically secured
controls.description: All media with cardholder data is physically secured.

controls.title: Classify media for sensitivity
controls.description: All media with cardholder data is classified in accordance with the sensitivity of the data.

controls.title: Maintain media inventory logs
controls.description: Inventory logs of all media with cardholder data are maintained.

controls.title: Destroy media when no longer needed
controls.description: Media with cardholder data is destroyed when it is no longer needed for business or legal reasons.

requirements.title: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs

controls.title: PCI DSS scope documented and validated
controls.description: PCI DSS scope is documented and confirmed by the entity at least once every 12 months.

controls.title: Incident response plan
controls.description: An incident response plan exists and is ready to be activated in the event of a suspected or confirmed security incident.

controls.title: Incident response plan reviewed and tested
controls.description: The security incident response plan is reviewed and tested, including all elements, at least annually.
